headline: Non-Communicable Diseases Account for 73% of All Malaysian Deaths
context: NCDs including heart disease, diabetes, and cancer caused 73% of deaths in Malaysia in 2025.

cards.big: 73% of Malaysian deaths in 2025 caused by non-communicable diseases mostly preventable
cards.sub: Heart disease diabetes cancer and respiratory illness dominate mortality statistics

cards.big: Diabetes prevalence in Malaysia at 18.3% is the highest in Southeast Asia
cards.sub: 3.9 million diagnosed diabetics with estimated 2 million more undiagnosed

cards.big: MOH spends only 2.8% of health budget on preventive care versus 85% on treatment
cards.sub: Funding imbalance treats disease after onset rather than preventing it

cards.big: Finland reduced NCD mortality 65% over 30 years through population-wide prevention programs
cards.sub: North Karelia Project demonstrated that sustained prevention investment saves lives and money

cards.big: Spending 85% on treatment and 3% on prevention is like buying fire trucks but refusing smoke detectors
Should Malaysia allocate at least 10% of health budget to NCD prevention programs
